_meta.notes: Elven homeland. Built on tidal mudflats east of the metropolis; every street is a pontoon and every pontoon is owned. The four sub-populations have their own quarters but the borders are flexible by hour, by tide, and by which contract has just been signed. Mudglass is the local high-end material; estuary mud fired into prismatic plates.

tagline: Pontoons over mudflat. Every street is owned. Every conversation is a negotiation.
ambientPool: A Branchman's slate is written, erased, and rewritten three times in the time it takes to walk past it.
The smell of estuary mud is strong at low tide — sharp, mineral, almost iodine.
A Salt-folk wholesaler shouts a price across the pontoon and gets the answer back at half-volume from someone walking the other way.
The act is at full performance in the public street; through any window you can hear it dropped.
A pontoon shifts under your weight by exactly the amount that says somebody important built it.

rooms.summary: The main thoroughfare. A mile of plank causeway over mudflat, lined with elven counting houses and contract-rooms. The boards have been rewritten so many times they no longer creak the same way twice.
ambientPool: An elf in unspoiled ivory walks past, the act so practiced you can't tell it's an act.
Two Vellum nod once at each other — they have just signed the same client to opposing positions.
A child runs along the pontoon shouting a tide warning. Three counting houses immediately close their shutters.
Someone has chalked a number into a doorpost that wasn't there an hour ago.

exits.narrative: Into the information broker's offices, marked by a closed door with no sign.
Out toward the mudflats where the kilns work.

rooms.summary: An elven banking pontoon. Long counters, slate boards, cushioned chairs for clients. The act is absolute here — every gesture commercial.
ambientPool: A Branchman pours tea with mathematical precision. The tea's temperature is a courtesy that costs you nothing yet, but soon will.
A clerk's pen makes the same mark twice in a row. Both are intentional.
Outside, the tide turns. The pontoon settles a finger's width.
An elderly Branchman looks at you for the first time in the meeting and the act drops for just a moment, then comes back.

rooms.summary: Information brokers' den. Heavy curtains, a desk facing the door, a second desk in the back where the actual filing happens. No price list. The air smells of ink and tea.
ambientPool: A Clearseer raises a single hand to indicate they are listening but not yet billing you.
A pigeon arrives at a back window with a paper tied to its leg. The Clearseer doesn't move.
Three slips of paper are passed across the desk in different directions in the space of a minute.
The act is thicker here than anywhere — it is what they sell.

rooms.summary: Out on the mudflats themselves at low tide. Estuary mud is shoveled into kilns and fired into prismatic mudglass — the elves' high-end glazing. Hot work, dirty work, almost no act.
ambientPool: A Salt-folk laborer wipes mud off their forearm and laughs at something a fellow shouted.
A kiln-master cracks open a kiln; the mudglass inside catches three colors of light.
The tide is coming in. The kilns will be flooded by sundown. They are timed for it.
A Branchman has come down to inspect a shipment and is being teased openly by the kiln crew.

exits.narrative: Back inland to the main pontoon.
